title: Install @modelcontextprotocol/sdk and strip-ansi dependencies
body:
## Install New npm Dependencies

Add the two new runtime dependencies required for MCP terminal integration.

### Files to Modify
- `package.json` â€” add to `dependencies` section

### What to Add

```json
"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.0.0",
"strip-ansi": "^7.1.0"
```

- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` â€” Official MCP TypeScript SDK. Handles JSON-RPC framing, tool schemas, and stdio transport. Used by the MCP server process (`terminalMcpServer.ts`).
- `strip-ansi` â€” Well-maintained utility for stripping ANSI escape codes from terminal output. Used in `RingBuffer.readLines()` to return clean text to Claude.

Run `npm install` after updating `package.json`.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Both packages are in `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`) since they run in the packaged Electron app
- `npm install` completes without errors
- Both packages are importable in TypeScript without type errors

### Completion Goals
- [ ] `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` is listed in `package.json` `dependencies`
- [ ] `strip-ansi` is listed in `package.json` `dependencies`
- [ ] `npm install` runs successfully and `package-lock.json` is updated
- [ ] `import { Server } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js'` compiles without TS errors
- [ ] `import stripAnsi from 'strip-ansi'` compiles without TS errors
- [ ] The app still builds successfully (`npm run build` or equivalent) with the new dependencies

title: Implement RingBuffer class for terminal output capture
Integrate ring buffer into PTY module with readPtyBuffer export
body:
## Integrate Ring Buffer into PTY Module

Modify `src/main/pty.ts` to capture terminal output into per-PTY ring buffers and expose a read function.

### Files to Modify
- `src/main/pty.ts`

### Changes Required

#### 1. Add import at top of file
```typescript
import { RingBuffer } from './mcp/ringBuffer';
```

#### 2. Add module-level buffers Map (alongside existing `sessions` Map)
```typescript
const buffers = new Map<string, RingBuffer>();
```

#### 3. In `createPty()` â€” create buffer and capture data
After the existing `sessions.set(terminalId, ...)` call, add:
```typescript
buffers.set(terminalId, new RingBuffer());
```

In the existing `proc.onData()` handler (which currently calls `window.webContents.send("pty-data", ...)`), add the buffer append **before** sending to renderer:
```typescript
proc.onData((data) => {
  buffers.get(terminalId)?.append(data);  // ADD THIS LINE
  window.webContents.send("pty-data", terminalId, data);
});
```

#### 4. In `killPty()` â€” clean up buffer
After `sessions.delete(terminalId)`, add:
```typescript
buffers.delete(terminalId);
```

#### 5. Export `readPtyBuffer()` function
Add this new exported function:
```typescript
export function readPtyBuffer(terminalId: string, lines: number): string | undefined {
  const buf = buffers.get(terminalId);
  if (!buf) return undefined;
  return buf.readLines(lines);
}
```
Returns `undefined` when no buffer exists (PTY never created or already killed), so callers can distinguish "terminal not found" from "terminal has no output".

### Acceptance Criteria
- When a PTY receives data (simulated via `proc.onData()`), the buffer captures it
- `readPtyBuffer(terminalId, 50)` returns the last 50 lines of captured output, ANSI-stripped
- `readPtyBuffer` returns `undefined` for unknown `terminalId`
- `killPty()` removes the buffer from the Map (no memory leak)
- Existing `proc.onData()` â†’ renderer behavior is unchanged

### Completion Goals
- [ ] `src/main/pty.ts` imports `RingBuffer` from `./mcp/ringBuffer`
- [ ] A new module-level `buffers` Map (`Map<string, RingBuffer>`) is declared
- [ ] `createPty()` creates a `RingBuffer` instance and stores it in the `buffers` Map for each new PTY
- [ ] Every `proc.onData()` callback appends data to the corresponding ring buffer before sending to the renderer
- [ ] `killPty()` deletes the buffer entry from the Map alongside the existing session cleanup
- [ ] `readPtyBuffer(terminalId, lines)` is exported and returns the ANSI-stripped last N lines, or `undefined` if no buffer exists
- [ ] The existing PTY data flow to the renderer (`window.webContents.send("pty-data", ...)`) is unaffected
- [ ] The file compiles without TypeScript errors

title: Create terminal registry for name-to-ID resolution in main process
Wire renderer-to-main IPC sync for terminal registry
body:
## Wire Terminal Registry Sync (Renderer â†’ Main via IPC)

Set up the IPC channel that keeps the main process terminal registry up-to-date with the renderer's `terminalStore` state. The renderer pushes updates whenever terminals are added/removed; the main process stores them in `terminalRegistry`.

### Files to Modify

#### 1. `src/types.ts` â€” Extend `TraceAPI` interface
Add to the `TraceAPI` interface:
```typescript
syncWorkspaceTerminals(workspaceId: string, terminals: TerminalTab[]): Promise<void>;
```
Import `TerminalTab` from `'../stores/terminalStore'` if not already imported.

#### 2. `src/preload.ts` â€” Expose the IPC call
Add to the `traceAPI` object exposed via `contextBridge`:
```typescript
syncWorkspaceTerminals: (workspaceId: string, terminals: TerminalTab[]) =>
  ipcRenderer.invoke('sync-workspace-terminals', workspaceId, terminals),
```

#### 3. `src/main/ipc.ts` â€” Register IPC handler
Add a new handler (follow the existing `ipcMain.handle` pattern):
```typescript
ipcMain.handle('sync-workspace-terminals', (_event, workspaceId: string, terminals: TerminalTab[]) => {
  updateWorkspaceTerminals(workspaceId, terminals);
  return { success: true };
});
```
Import `{ updateWorkspaceTerminals }` from `'./mcp/terminalRegistry'`.

#### 4. `src/stores/terminalStore.ts` â€” Trigger sync after mutations
The goal is to call `window.traceAPI.syncWorkspaceTerminals(workspaceId, terminals)` after any action that modifies `_allTerminals`. This is fire-and-forget (no need to await).

The cleanest approach is a Zustand `subscribe()` call outside the store definition:

```typescript
// After the store is created, add outside the store definition:
useTerminalStore.subscribe((state) => {
  const wsId = state.currentWorkspaceId; // track which workspace is selected
  if (!wsId || !window.traceAPI) return;
  const terminals = state._allTerminals.get(wsId) ?? [];
  void window.traceAPI.syncWorkspaceTerminals(wsId, terminals);
});
```

Alternatively (and more reliably since all workspaces need syncing, not just the active one), call `window.traceAPI.syncWorkspaceTerminals` at the end of each mutating action in the store: `initializeDefaults`, `addTerminal`, `killTerminal`, `rerunTab`, `stopTab`, `onPtyExit`. The IPC call is fire-and-forget.

Pick whichever approach fits more naturally with the existing store structure. The subscribe approach is simpler but only syncs the active workspace. The per-action approach syncs all workspaces.

### Acceptance Criteria
- After `initializeDefaults()` runs in the renderer, the main process registry contains the workspace's terminals
- After `addTerminal()` runs in the renderer, the main process registry is updated
- `window.traceAPI.syncWorkspaceTerminals` is a no-op if called before the window is initialized (guard against null)
- No synchronous blocking: the IPC call is fire-and-forget with `.catch()` or `void`

### Completion Goals
- [ ] `syncWorkspaceTerminals` method is added to the `TraceAPI` interface in `src/types.ts`
- [ ] `syncWorkspaceTerminals` is exposed via `contextBridge` in `src/preload.ts` using `ipcRenderer.invoke('sync-workspace-terminals', ...)`
- [ ] A `sync-workspace-terminals` IPC handler is registered in `src/main/ipc.ts` that calls `updateWorkspaceTerminals()` from the terminal registry
- [ ] The terminal store in `src/stores/terminalStore.ts` triggers the sync IPC call whenever its terminal state changes (via `subscribe()` or per-action calls)
- [ ] The sync call is fire-and-forget â€” it does not block the store action or cause re-renders
- [ ] The sync call is guarded against `window.traceAPI` being undefined (e.g., during SSR or before preload)
- [ ] All modified files compile without TypeScript errors

title: Build MCP bridge Unix socket server in Electron main process
Create standalone MCP server process with stdio transport and Vite entry point
Integrate MCP bridge lifecycle and --mcp-config into agent spawn
Add renderer-side create_terminal support with store action and IPC listener
Write unit tests for RingBuffer, terminal registry, and MCP bridge
